mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent deflationary pressure, indicated by CPIAUCSL below 200, reduces real interest rates and boosts equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL remains below 200.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payrolls (PAYEMS) and declining unemployment (UNRATE) signals a tightening labor market, increasing corporate earnings visibility and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows negative 5d returns in 50%+ of cases where both PAYEMS exceeds 135k and UNRATE falls below 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in industrial production (INDPRO) combined with rising housing starts (HOUST) indicate supply-side bottlenecks, creating inflationary pressures that depress equity valuations over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in 60%+ of instances where INDPRO declines and HOUST rises.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth (GDPC1) in the prior quarter predicts sustained corporate earnings momentum, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through 2022 Q2.
failure_condition: SPX shows negative 20d returns in 50%+ of quarters where GDPC1 growth exceeds 3%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer spending (RSAFS) above 366 billion signals strong demand-side momentum, increasing corporate revenues and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in 60%+ of instances where RSAFS exceeds 366166.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising Fed funds rates (FEDFUNDS) and declining housing starts (HOUST) indicates monetary tightening suppressing real estate demand, creating negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows positive 20d returns in 50%+ of cases where FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0 and HOUST falls below 1665.

mechanism_narrative: A labor market overheating scenario, defined by UNRATE below 4.0% and PAYEMS growth above 135k, creates wage inflation risks that depress equity multiples and generate negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in 50%+ of instances where UNRATE < 4.0 and PAYEMS > 135613.
